concerns.analysis: The output contains severe AI hallucination and 'thought process' leakage directly injected into the body text. On page 23, the model inserted a lengthy internal monologue discussing its extraction process, including non-English characters. Similarly, on page 38, the model injected a Bengali word and JSON-like artifacts into a sentence.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: For landfills located in geographical areas with a 30-year annual average precipitation of less than 25 inches, as measured at the nearest representative official meteorological site, the k value to be used is 0.02 per year.
concerns.output_quote:
the k value to be used is 0.02 per year मजदूर . 

 Wait, wait. "0.02 per year."

Wait, I must check my text again. Oh, "0.02 per year."
I will re-write carefully without any extra characters from thought process.
Actually let's just supply the raw string directly.

Here is the correct structure.

concerns.analysis: The extraction completely failed for two pages (Page 43 and Page 60) due to an 'Empty response from Gemini' error. The content of both pages is entirely missing from the output. Page 60, for example, contains critical permit conditions LL through OO, which are entirely absent in the converted JSON.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
LL. Reporting Requirements for Non-Operating Equipment [15A NCAC 02Q .0508(i)(16)]
The Permittee shall maintain a record of operation for permitted equipment noting whenever the equipment is taken from and placed into operation.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains the 'Seal of the State of North Carolina' prominently at the top center of the first page. In the rendered HTML, this meaningful government seal is missing and has been replaced by a text placeholder instead of a proper `` element. Because this specific seal does not appear anywhere else in the document, its absence constitutes a missing meaningful image.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Seal of the State of North Carolina image located at the top center
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Seal of the State of North Carolina]

concerns.analysis: The conversion severely flattens nested list structures, causing sub-items to lose their hierarchical meaning and appear as top-level sibling items. For example, on page 6 of the PDF, item 'g.' has a sub-item 'ii.' which in turn contains sub-items '(A)' and '(B)'. It is followed by 'iii.' and 'iv.' which belong to 'g.'. In the extraction output, '(A)', '(B)', 'iii.', and 'iv.' are all placed at the very root level of the list array, functioning as siblings to 'g.' rather than nested children. Th
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
g. The facility’s collection and control system that captures the gas generated within the landfill shall meet the following requirements:
[...]
ii. Collect gas from each area, cell, or group of cells in the landfill in which the initial solid waste has been placed for a period of
(A) 5 years or more if active; or
(B) 2 years or more if closed or at final grade.
iii. Collect gas at a sufficient extraction rate; and
iv. Be designed to minimize off-site migration of subsurface gas.
concerns.location: Source PDF Page 6 / Output JSON Page 10

concerns.analysis: On page 31 of the output (corresponding to page 27 of the source PDF), the exact same text block containing paragraph (a) and its child item (1) is repeated consecutively 6 times in the list array. The source PDF only has this text appear once.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: "(a) Except as provided in 40 CFR 62.16724(d)(2), the specified methods in paragraphs (a)(1) through (6) of this section must be used to determine whether the gas collection system is in compliance with 40 CFR 62.16714(b)(2)."
concerns.output_quote: The list item starting with "(a) Except as provided in 40 CFR 62.16724(d)(2)..." and its nested child "(1) For the purposes of calculating..." is duplicated 6 times in the list items array.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 428 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=428, examples=['mailto:massoud.eslambolchi@deq.nc.gov', 'https://www.oah.nc.gov/hearings-division/hearing-process/fil', 'https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-40/part-62/subpart-OOO']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (74): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; Permit No. 08849T09; Page 3
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=74, pages_affected=53/60. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 3 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://www.oah.nc.gov/hearings-division/filing/hearing-forms.; (https://www.epa.gov/electronic-reporting-air-emissions/compliance-and-emissions; (https://www3.epa.gov/ttn/chief/cedri/index.html).
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=3. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "This list is not intended to be a detailed record ". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (3 instance(s)): p10→p11: ordered list J→K continues; p12→p13: list item ends “…ure gauge pressure in the gas collection”, continues as paragraph; p16→p17: ordered list X→Y continues
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=3. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 3 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Permittee:", "AOS", "Emission Source ID No."
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=3. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 426 link(s) clustered at bottom of 28 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=28, total_displaced=426. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 2, Page 3, Page 5
Repeated footer/header text (2x): "[86 fr 27770, may 21, 2021, as amended at 87 fr 8203, feb. 1"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
